skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Render videos from React/Remotion TSX code to MP4 via inference.sh cloud service
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: All 40 static findings are false positives. The skill uses inference.sh API for cloud-based video rendering. External command patterns are documentation examples shown to users, not skill-executed code. Network calls are legitimate references to the inference.sh rendering service. No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Command Examples
low_findings.description: The skill documentation shows users how to run infsh commands via Bash. This is intended behavior - users copy these examples to execute video renders. Not a security issue.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks and code blocks are markdown documentation formatting. Users run these commands themselves, not the skill. This is the documented usage pattern for inference.sh apps.

low_findings.title: External Service References
low_findings.description: The skill references inference.sh URLs for the cloud rendering service. This is the expected architecture - the skill calls inference.sh API to render videos.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: References to inference.sh and remotion.dev are legitimate service URLs. The skill is designed to use inference.sh as the rendering backend.

content.value_statement: Transform React/Remotion TSX code into MP4 videos programmatically. Generate animations, motion graphics, and data-driven videos without manual video editing tools.
actual_capabilities: Render MP4 videos from React/Remotion TSX component code
Support all Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate
Configure video resolution, FPS, duration, and codec
Create animated text, counters, and sequence animations
Pass dynamic props to rendered components
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed
Video rendering happens on remote inference.sh servers
Complex animations may require longer render times
Requires internet connection for cloud rendering

use_cases.title: Developer Creates Animated Content
use_cases.description: Generate video content programmatically for social media, demos, or tutorials using React code

use_cases.title: Data Visualization to Video
use_cases.description: Convert animated charts and data visualizations into video format for presentations

use_cases.title: Automated Video Production
use_cases.description: Generate videos from templates with dynamic data like scores, stats, or user content

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with the text 'Hello World' fading in over 3 seconds at 30fps in 1920x1080 resolution using Remotion

prompt_templates.title: Animated Counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video that counts from 0 to 100 over 5 seconds at 60fps showing progress with large white numbers on black background

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Sequence Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with three text elements appearing in sequence: 'First', 'Second', 'Third' each fading in at different times using Remotion Sequence component

prompt_templates.title: Custom Props Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Remotion video that accepts props for title and subtitle, rendering them centered on screen with specified colors

output_examples.input: Render a 3-second fade-in animation with 'Welcome' text
output_examples.output: Video file rendered at /tmp/output.mp4 with smooth fade-in animation showing 'Welcome' text

output_examples.input: Create a progress counter from 0-100% over 5 seconds
output_examples.output: MP4 video at /tmp/counter.mp4 showing animated percentage increasing from 0 to 100

best_practices: Test your TSX code in Remotion Studio before using the skill for complex animations
Start with shorter durations (1-3 seconds) to verify the render works correctly
Use appropriate FPS based on your use case - 30fps for standard video, 60fps for smooth motion
anti_patterns: Do not pass extremely long TSX code strings - break complex videos into multiple renders
Avoid extremely high resolutions unless needed - 1920x1080 is standard for most use cases
Do not set duration exceeding 60 seconds without testing shorter versions first

faq.question: What is Remotion?
faq.answer: Remotion is a framework for creating videos programmatically using React. It lets you build video components using the same React APIs you use for web development.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI (infsh). Installation instructions are available via npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools

faq.question: Where does video rendering happen?
faq.answer: Video rendering occurs on inference.sh cloud servers. You send TSX code, and the service returns the rendered MP4 video.

faq.question: Can I use any React library?
faq.answer: You can use React and Remotion APIs. External libraries may not be available unless they're part of the Remotion runtime.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Rendering time depends on video complexity, duration, and server load. Simple 3-second videos typically render in under a minute.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs MP4 videos. You can configure codec, resolution, FPS, and duration via input parameters.
